rows.source_ref: Robopip, "Forex Mechanical System: SMA Crossover Pullback", babypips.com Trading Systems, June 5, 2015 (based on Big Pippin's Daily Chart Art framework). URL approx https://www.babypips.com/trading-systems/forex-mechanical-system-sma-crossover-pullback
rows.concept: Trade in the direction of the 100/200 SMA trend and time entries on a stochastic (14,3,3) pullback back into that trend.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 dual-SMA trend filter + stochastic pullback entry, deterministic, no ML, FX data available; but this HTML snapshot is truncated by a cookie-consent overlay so precise entry trigger, exit and SL/TP are missing.
rows.notes: Only the intro survived the page capture: indicators = 100 & 200 SMA + Stochastic (14,3,3); direction from SMA crossover, entry on stochastic pullback. Generic MA-crossover trend-follow (heavily arbitraged retail template, contradicts FX-edge doctrine). Timeframe D1 inferred from 'Daily Chart Art'. Full rules would need reconstruction from the original babypips article.

notes: PDF is a single-page babypips.com HTML snapshot; content truncated by a German cookie-consent overlay after the intro paragraph.
Extracted via pypdf and pdftotext -layout; both yield identical truncated text. No parameter table or exit rules present in the capture.
